prevalence.trend: stable; high antibiotic use in some settings

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical criteria; bacterial suggested by persistent/severe/double-worsening patterns; imaging rarely needed

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with symptomatic care; stewardship is variable

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinically diagnosed; limited imaging; antibiotics frequently overused
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Symptomatic therapy and selective antibiotics; manage allergic rhinitis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse and limited ENT access
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost symptomatic care and clear clinical criteria can reduce unnecessary antibiotics
